title: Correcting the Binary Search Implementation with Edge Cases

statement.markdown: Implement a binary search function that finds an element in a sorted array. The function should return the index of the element if found, otherwise, return -1. Handle the following edge cases: an empty array, an array with a single element, and an array with duplicate elements. Consider the case where the target element is present at the beginning or end of the array.
statement.inputFormat: A sorted array of integers and a target integer to search for.
statement.outputFormat: The index of the target element if found, otherwise -1.
statement.notes: The binary search function should have a time complexity of O(log n), where n is the size of the array.

examples.explanation: The target element 1 is present at index 2 in the array.
The target element 5 is present at index 0 in the array.
The target element 10 is not present in the empty array.

hints.text: Consider the case where the array has duplicate elements and the target element is present at the beginning or end of the array.

editorial.title: Correcting the Binary Search Implementation
editorial.intuition: The binary search function can be implemented using a loop that finds the middle element of the array and compares it with the target element.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach can be implemented using a linear search function that iterates over the array and finds the target element.
editorial.betterSolution: The binary search function can be optimized to handle edge cases by considering the case where the array has duplicate elements and the target element is present at the beginning or end of the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves implementing a binary search function with a time complexity of O(log n), where n is the size of the array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The binary search function can be proven correct by showing that it satisfies the conditions of the problem and handles edge cases correctly.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the binary search function can be performed to ensure that it handles edge cases correctly and returns the correct index of the target element.

functionSignature.java: public int binarySearch(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def binarySearch(self, nums, target: int) -> int:
